# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 930
- **Lines of code**: 312,109
- **Languages**: TypeScript (778), CSS (42), JSON (30), JavaScript (29), Markdown (25)
- **Zones**: 39 (39 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-28T01:34:30.222Z

## Architecture Zones

### Agent Core (`agent-core`)

Primary autonomous agent implementation including analysis, review, and execution logic for task processing.

- Files: 130
- Cohesion: 0.99, Coupling: 0.01
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/shell.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/agent-core/context.md`

### Application Shell Components (`application-shell-components`)

Main application entry point and various overlay UI components including search, FAQ, and status indicators.

- Files: 10
- Cohesion: 0.20, Coupling: 0.80
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/application-shell-components/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit
GET     /api/hench/concurrency
GET     /api/hench/config
GET     /api/hench/execute/status
GET     /api/hench/execute/status/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory
GET     /api/hench/memory/history
GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory/leaks
GET     /api/hench/metrics
GET     /api/hench/metrics/snapshots

GET     /api/hench/runs/health

POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- Heavy cross-zone coupling (web-16 → web: 13 imports, web-24 → web-20: 9 imports) indicates missing abstraction layers between implementation details [critical]
- Missing shared visualization interface abstraction forces chart and navigation zones to couple directly to different foundation systems [critical]
- Multiple service layers (queue, validation) exceed expected coupling thresholds for isolated components, indicating insufficient architectural boundaries [warning]
- Token usage views mixed into polling infrastructure zone instead of being consolidated with other usage analytics functionality [warning]
- UI zone organization lacks consistent abstraction strategy - general utilities scattered across domain zones while application views leak into foundation layer [warning]
- View components scattered across utility zones instead of grouped in dedicated view/page architectural layer [warning]
- Web-16 zone imports heavily from multiple zones (13+8 imports) suggesting it occupies wrong architectural layer or needs interface abstraction [warning]
- God function: PRDView in packages/web/src/viewer/views/prd.ts calls 83 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- Client-server architectural boundary is well-maintained except for schema-infrastructure zone violation [warning]
- Cross-cutting performance concerns are integrated into functional zones rather than separated into performance layers [info]
- Domain boundary success varies dramatically: hench achieves clean layered isolation while web shows architectural sprawl across 29 zones [warning]
- Error-recovery zone demonstrates how focused responsibility leads to excellent architectural metrics (0.64 cohesion, 0.36 coupling) [info]
- Foundation anti-pattern where ui-foundation contains both infrastructure utilities and application-specific views [warning]
- Functional zone clustering (polling, performance, visualization) suggests opportunities for architectural consolidation [info]
- Hook-component co-location pattern provides good separation between state management and presentation across zones [info]
- Hub-and-spoke architecture with UI zones radiating from different foundation systems creates clean separation but misses opportunities for shared abstractions [info]
- Inconsistent service abstraction patterns across utility zones - some achieve clean boundaries while others leak implementation details to consumers [warning]
- Inconsistent use of abstraction patterns (hooks vs direct coupling) across similar UI zones indicates need for architectural standardization [warning]
- ... and 4 more

### Relationships

- Consistent external dependencies on timer/polling infrastructure reveal foundational services that support monitoring and optimization layers [info]
- Multi-level hub architecture with web-platform as primary hub and multiple secondary hubs creates complex dependency hierarchies [warning]
- No direct inter-zone dependencies between these UI zones suggests good boundary definition, but shared interface opportunities are missed [info]
- Performance optimization zones show clean complementary design without circular dependencies [info]
- Visualization concerns fragmented across multiple zones without clear abstraction hierarchy [warning]
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 133 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- High cohesion (0.99) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (agent-core)
- High coupling (0.8) — 13 imports target "web-platform" [warning] (application-shell-components)
- Low cohesion (0.2) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (application-shell-components)
- High coupling (0.71) — 2 imports target "ui-foundation" [warning] (chart-visualization-layer)
- Low cohesion (0.29) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (chart-visualization-layer)
- High cohesion (0.9) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (command-validation)
- High coupling (0.73) — 2 imports target "interactive-data-visualization" [warning] (dashboard-visualization)
- High coupling (0.65) — 2 imports target "schema-infrastructure" [warning] (data-visualization)
- Low cohesion (0.35) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (data-visualization)
- High cohesion (0.88) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (execution-queue)
- ... and 30 more

### Suggestions

- Five separate analysis services could be consolidated into fewer, more cohesive analysis modules to reduce internal complexity [warning] (agent-core)
- Critical architectural risk: 0.2/0.8 cohesion/coupling ratio indicates zone will break with any significant change [warning] (application-shell-components)
- Entry point misplacement: main.ts should be in dedicated application-bootstrap zone, not mixed with UI components [info] (application-shell-components)
- Severe naming convention violation: no files contain 'shell' or 'application' prefixes despite zone name - rename zone to reflect actual contents or standardize file naming [warning] (application-shell-components)
- Critical risk zone: 0.29 cohesion + 0.71 coupling exceeds fragility threshold - any change will likely cascade across multiple unrelated files [warning] (chart-visualization-layer)
- Zone name 'chart-visualization-layer' implies architectural layer but contains concrete implementations - rename to 'chart-components' or similar implementation-focused name [info] (chart-visualization-layer)
- contracts.ts pattern should be adopted by other service zones (execution-queue) for consistent interface definition [info] (command-validation)
- Most fragile zone in codebase (0.27/0.73) - requires immediate consolidation or complete redesign [warning] (dashboard-visualization)
- Zero test coverage for critical dashboard components creates significant quality and maintenance risk [warning] (dashboard-visualization)
- Zone shows fragile architecture (0.35/0.65) requiring careful change management to prevent degradation [warning] (data-visualization)
- ... and 60 more
